title: Whole user-space parity recovery and authority cutover defined
owner: Governance Board / LifeOS Data Agent / QA Agent
objective: Define the staged migration that proves the entire user-space is represented, reconstructable, recoverable, and operated from PostgreSQL before one explicit owner-approved authority switch retires flat files as editable authority, while retaining preserved history and projections. Authority is kept unchanged; this task only defines the plan, its parity gate, and its rollback.

work_items.decision: Enumerate the entire user-space as 14 switching classes (files, directories, code modules, configuration, planning tables, communications, application state, media/blob references, identities, permissions, history, relationships, semantic embeddings, envctl migration state) plus 4 non-switching classes (transient redb, per-agent cognition, derived projections, external secrets). Every class is assigned exactly one canonical target owner and one authority_action per STORE-001; the two current durable redb uses (PGREDB-001, PGREDB-002) are named explicitly so none is silently reclassified.

Bind each of the 14 gate clauses to a concrete parity dimension PD-01..PD-14 (entity_counts, identities, bytes, metadata, relationships, permissions, history, semantic_retrieval, behavior, reconstruction, backup, restore, failback, observed_lifeos_operation), each with a check, an evidence artifact, and a pass criterion.

Sequence the migration as five staged steps with entry/exit gates: (1) parity decomposition, (2) restore rehearsal, (3) staged dual-read, (4) explicit switch, (5) retention decision. Flat files remain the editable authority through Stages 1-3.

Permit exactly one explicit owner-approved authority switch (Stage 4), and only after all 14 parity dimensions pass and the STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant is satisfied (green live blobstore_parity.rs for durable classes; RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation for the semantic class). At switch, flat files become the read-only preserved reference and the envctl-derived projection.

Require backup, restore, and failback to be rehearsed on a disposable namespace before the switch, with both representations retained, so the recovery envelope is proven prior to any authority change.
Retain the preserved source (read-only) until a separate explicit retention approval; this task never deletes source, history, or any superseded cutover namespace.

On any parity failure, unapproved switch, or post-switch instability, fail back to preserved source authority, stop database writes, and retain the failed cutover namespace and its proof without deleting either representation.

store_001_alignment: PostgreSQL is the single durable writer; retiring flat files as editable authority is building to the intended blueprint spec (an upgrade), preserved via the BlobStore SHA-256 verified-cutover mechanism and the byte-identical envctl projection path.
completion_gate: generated/postgres_authority_cutover_plan.json defines all 14 parity dimensions bound to the gate clauses, every user-space class assigned exactly one owner and authority_action, five migration stages, exactly one explicit authority switch gated on full parity, a retention policy that keeps source available until approval, and a failback rollback; proof_records/POSTGRES-010.proof.json records zero product execution and authority unchanged.
next_action: Keep authority unchanged; after graph closure execute parity decomposition, restore rehearsal, staged dual-read, the explicit switch, and the retention decision.
